DEFENDANT SHIFTED THE BURDEN OF PROOF BASED ON FACTUALLY DEVOID DISCOVERY. THE COURT SUSTAINS OBJECTIONS TWO THROUGH FIFTEEN TO THE BROWN DECLARATION BASED ON D'AMICO AND HEARSAY. ALTHOUGH THE DEPOSITION QUESTIONS REGARDING ELLIOTT WERE FEW IN NUMBER, PLAINTIFF'S LACK OF KNOWLEDGE AS TO ELLIOTT WAS REAFFIRMED IN THE RESPONSE TO THE SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES. IN THESE CIRCUMSTANCES, STRIKING THE DECLARATION BASED ON D'AMICO IS WARRANTED, PARTICULARLY GIVEN PLAINTIFF'S ABILITY TO IDENTIFY OTHER PUMP MANUFACTURERS IN THE COURSE OF DISCOVERY. AMENDED MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Dec-01-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Dennis M. Young, Esq., of FOLEY & MANSFIELD (510-590-9500), appearing for defendant / moving party Campbell Industries
Oren P. Noah, Esq., of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Charley Brown

RULING:
DEFENDANT CAMPBELL INDUSTRIES' MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Argued and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Campbell Industries' motion for summary judgment and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to the first three issues (statute of limitations, workers' compensation exclusivity and identification of defendant in a non-employment capacity) and taken off calendar as to the last three issues (strict liability, false representation and punitive damages). Defendant did not satisfy its burden of production that plaintiff does not have asbestosis. Defendant's workers' compensation argument is not amenable to summary adjudication since it does not dispose of an entire claim. Plaintiff's non-employment identification of defendant is not speculative. Plaintiff dismissed his claims for strict liability and false representation and his request for punitive damages. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 = (220/HEK)

MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT BUFFALO PUMPS, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT.
APPEARANCES: JAMES P. NEVIN, ESQUIRE (BRAYTON PURCELL, 415.898.1555) APPEARING FOR PLAINTIFF. JAMES J. O'BRIEN, ESQUIRE (JACKSON & WALLACE, 415.982.6300) APPEARING FOR DEFENDANT.
RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: GRANTED. DEFENDANT SHIFTED THE BURDEN OF PROOF. PLAINTIFF FAILED TO CREATE A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT. THE FIRST SENTENCE OF PARAGRAPH SIX OF THE BROWN DECLARATION IS STRUCK AS HEARSAY. THE REMAINING OBJECTIONS TO THE BROWN DECLARATION ARE OVERRULED AS ARE THE OBJECTIONS TO BROWN'S DEPOSITION TESTIMONY. THE RECORD FAILS TO CREATE A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT THAT PLAINTIFF WAS EXPOSED TO ASBESTOS THAT WAS MANUFACTURED OR SOLD BY DEFENDANT. THE COURT PREVIOUSLY HAS REJECTED THE THEORY THAT DEFENDANT'S FAILURE TO WARN ABOUT THE USE OF OTHERS' ASBESTOS REPLACEMENT PARTS IS A BASIS FOR LIABILITY. DEFENDANT'S COUNSEL TO SUBMIT A FORM OF ORDER. (302/PJM/VC) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600

RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: GRANTED. DEFENDANT SHIFTED THE BURDEN OF PROOF BASED ON FACTUALLY DEVOID DISCOVERY. THE COURT SUSTAINS OBJECTIONS TWO THROUGH FIFTEEN TO THE BROWN DECLARATION BASED ON D'AMICO AND HEARSAY. ALTHOUGH THE DEPOSITION QUESTIONS REGARDING ELLIOTT WERE FEW IN NUMBER, PLAINTIFF'S LACK OF KNOWLEDGE AS TO ELLIOTT WAS REAFFIRMED IN THE RESPONSE TO THE SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES. IN THESE CIRCUMSTANCES, STRIKING THE DECLARATION BASED ON D'AMICO IS WARRANTED, PARTICULARLY GIVEN PLAINTIFF'S ABILITY TO IDENTIFY OTHER PUMP MANUFACTURERS IN THE COURSE OF DISCOVERY.
